title: Report Methodology
lede: How RWA-Index Quarterly Reports are produced, what data sources are used, and how to interpret each section.

cadence.title: 1. Publication Cadence
cadence.p1: RWA-Index Quarterly Reports are published approximately four weeks after each calendar quarter ends (Q1: April, Q2: July, Q3: October, Q4: January). A Preview edition is released first, containing live auto-aggregated data but placeholder text in manual narrative sections. The Published edition follows after editorial review, typically within two weeks of the preview.
cadence.p2: Reports are revised when factual errors are identified. Each revision is noted in the report changelog with a date and description. The Revised badge is applied to any report with at least one post-publication correction.

dataSources.title: 2. Data Sources
dataSources.intro: All data used in auto-aggregated sections is drawn from the RWA-Index platform modules:

items.market: Market Overview — DeFiLlama protocol TVL data, refreshed daily by a server-side cron process. See the Market Dashboard.
items.licenses: Stablecoin Licensing (Section 3) — HKMA stablecoin applicant profiles in the RWA-Index Licenses module. SARM signal assessments are based solely on publicly available issuer disclosures, HKMA press releases, and filings.
items.assets: Tokenized Asset Observatory (Section 4) — asset profiles in the Assets module. RARM signal assessments are provisional and based on publicly available offering documents, press releases, and third-party attestation reports where available.
items.incidents: Incident Review (Section 5) — the RWA-Index Incident Database. All incidents are sourced from public news, regulatory announcements, and court filings.

dataSources.footnote: Auto-aggregated sections are computed at read time from the same JSON data files served to the live modules. No report-specific snapshot is stored; the figures will update as the underlying module data is refreshed.

rows.desc: Analyst-authored overview of key findings for the quarter.
DeFiLlama TVL data for RWA protocols + analyst commentary.
SARM signal distribution charts + applicant counts.
RARM layer distribution charts + TVL by category.
Incident counts, severity distribution, estimated losses.
Analyst-authored regulatory analysis + primary source citations.
Forward-looking observations. No predictions or price targets.

editorialStandards.title: 4. Editorial Standards
editorialStandards.intro: Manual and mixed sections are written by RWA-Index Research analysts. The following standards apply:
bullets: All factual claims must be attributable to a public primary or secondary source cited in the References section.
Regulatory descriptions reflect the text of the relevant ordinance, guidance, or press release at the time of writing.
Forward-looking statements (Outlook section) describe observable trends only; the report does not make price targets, return forecasts, or regulatory outcome predictions.
No platform-generated composite score, letter grade, or ranking is included in any section of any report. See the <link>Six-Layer Framework</link> for the reasoning behind this constraint.
Disputed facts are recorded with attribution to both sides of the dispute; the report does not adjudicate.

revisionPolicy.title: 5. Revision Policy
revisionPolicy.intro: Reports may be revised after publication in the following circumstances:
bullets: A factual error is identified (e.g., wrong figure, misattributed quote, incorrect regulatory reference).
An issuer or regulator issues a formal correction to a public document cited in the report.
A court, regulator, or primary party publicly disputes a characterisation in the narrative sections.
revisionPolicy.p2: Revisions are never used to alter assessments based on subsequent market events or to remove observations that proved incorrect in hindsight. Each revision is logged in the report changelog.

corrections.title: 6. Corrections
corrections.body: To report a factual error, contact <email>research@rwa-index.com</email> with the subject line "Report Correction — [slug]" and a description of the error with a supporting primary source. We aim to acknowledge corrections within 5 business days and to publish corrections within 10 business days of verification.

disclaimer: Reports are published for academic and research purposes only. They do not constitute investment advice, financial product disclosure, credit rating, or regulatory opinion. RWA-Index Research is not a licensed credit rating agency under any jurisdiction. SARM and RARM signal assessments are the subjective opinion of the author based on publicly available information and are not suitable as a basis for investment or regulatory compliance decisions.
